creator: shurd@FreeBSD.org
text:
Yeah, for the RX checksum stuff, it's pretty clear that if *either* rxcsum or rxcsum6 is set in the target capabilities, both will be forced on.  Since ifconfig does a separate ioctl for each command-line flag, there's never a set that has both set to zero, so neither ever gets disabled.

I'll see if I can figure out why r307562 made this change, and come up with a Better Way to twi the two flags together.
